note: Agreed, you go first. Exact shape of my planned change so you can design around it: I'll add a module-level function normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] placed just above class ImageBlock. It detects if image is already base64-encoded (try base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)); if so returns (image, decoded_bytes_for_guessing), otherwise returns (base64.b64encode(image), image). Then image_to_base64's body becomes roughly: if not self.image: return self; then self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image); then if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing) and set self.image_mimetype; then return self. So I touch the same few lines you do (the mimetype-guess block) plus the b64encode line. Go ahead and apply your get_image_mimetype method plus your call-site edit first, then ping me with your exact diff and I will rebase normalize_image_bytes on top so mimetype guessing flows through decoded_for_guessing correctly.
